core_model.structure_summary: Minimization over a feasible set of an empirical (finite-sample) average of a convex random objective, analyzed as a surrogate for minimizing its expectation, with high-probability and expectation bounds on the optimality gap.

variables.meaning: decision vector in a convex feasible set
expected value of a convex random function, approximated by its sample average
i.i.d. random samples entering the objective
strong-convexity modulus, gradient-Lipschitz and moment/variance constants, target accuracy and confidence

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Minimization of a (strongly) convex objective over a convex feasible set, characterized by first-order optimality of the true expected-value objective.

notes: The paper's central object is the sample-average-approximation optimization problem (minimize empirical mean of a convex random function) and its sample-complexity bounds relative to the expected-value program; Regularized (Bregman/prox) variant appears but the core is the SAA optimization. Skeleton is the underlying deterministic convex program min_x E[f(x,xi)].
